name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications
description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications means your body isn’t using insulin properly, so sugar builds up in your blood, but it hasn’t yet caused damage to organs or tissues. It’s like your house’s thermostat not responding well—the heater (insulin) still works, but the rooms (your cells) aren’t “listening,” so the heat (sugar) keeps rising. Things still function, but if it continues unchecked, the system can start wearing out.

name: Hyperlipidemia, unspecified
description: Hyperlipidemia means there’s too much fat—like cholesterol or triglycerides—floating around in your blood. It’s like having too much grease in your plumbing; things still flow, but over time that buildup can start to clog the pipes. You might not feel it day-to-day, but it quietly raises the risk of heart disease and stroke if left unchecked.

name: Major depressive disorder, single episode, unspecified
description: Major depressive disorder is when sadness and loss of interest stick around so long and so deeply that they start affecting how you think, sleep, and live day-to-day. It’s like your brain’s “brightness dial” gets turned way down—everything feels dimmer, heavier, and harder to move through. The lights still work, but it takes real effort (and often help) to turn them back up again.

name: Essential (primary) hypertension
description: Essential (primary) hypertension is when your blood pressure stays high without a single clear cause—it usually builds up over years from genetics, aging, and lifestyle. It’s like your home’s water pipes slowly getting more pressure because the pump keeps working a little too hard and the pipes get a bit tighter over time. Nothing’s broken, but if it keeps up too long, those pipes can start to wear down.

name: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, unspecified
description: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) means the airways in your lungs are damaged and narrowed, making it hard to breathe over time. It’s like trying to breathe through a straw that’s partly clogged—air still moves, but it takes more effort and never feels like quite enough. The damage builds up slowly, usually from smoking or long-term irritation, and can’t be fully undone once it’s there.

name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease, Without esophhagitis
description:
GERD is a chronic condition in which stomach acid or contents flow backward from the stomach into the esophagus, causing symptoms such as heartburn, regurgitation, and possible injury to the esophageal lining. Your stomach makes acid to help break down food, but sometimes that acid sneaks up into the tube (your esophagus) that connects your mouth to your stomach.When that happens, it can burn or taste sour in your throat.
It’s like when you shake a soda can too much and the bubbles shoot out the top — your stomach acid is the bubbles, and your esophagus gets messy from the splash.

name: Anxiety
description:
Anxiety is a mental and physical response characterized by excessive worry, nervousness, or fear that is out of proportion to a situation. Our brain has a tiny helper that rings a bell when it thinks something scary might happen.When you have anxiety, that helper rings the bell too much, even when you’re safe.
It’s like having a fire alarm that keeps going off just because you made toast — it’s trying to protect you, but it’s too sensitive.

name: Osteoarthritis
description:
Osteoarthritis is a degenerative joint disease characterized by the breakdown of articular cartilage and changes in the underlying bone, leading to pain, stiffness, decreased mobility, and sometimes swelling. It is the most common form of arthritis and is typically caused by aging, joint injury, or repetitive stress. Your joints are like door hinges that help your body move smoothly.
Inside those hinges is a soft, slippery padding called cartilage that keeps the bones from rubbing together.
When you get osteoarthritis, that padding wears down — like a rusty door hinge that squeaks and grinds when you open it.

name: Obesity, Unspecified
description:
Obesity is defined as an excessive accumulation of body fat that poses a health risk, typically identified by a body mass index of 30.0 or higher. Obesity means your body is carrying more fat than it needs to stay healthy.
It can make your heart, joints, and other organs work harder than they should.
It’s like a car built to carry four people that’s always loaded with eight — it still runs, but the engine and tires wear out faster.

name: Unspecified asthma, uncomplicated
description:
Asthma is a chronic inflammatory disease of the airways characterized by episodes of wheezing, shortness of breath, chest tightness, and coughing. Asthma means your breathing tubes inside your lungs sometimes get tight and irritated, making it hard to breathe.You might cough, wheeze, or feel like you can’t get enough air, especially when you’re sick, exercising, or around things like smoke or dust.
It’s like trying to breathe through a straw that keeps shrinking whenever the air gets dusty or cold — medicine helps open it back up so air can flow easily again.
